title: Graphic Designer
description:
In-house Graphic Designer

The Theatre Practice’s (Practice) Communications & Partnerships Team advocates the value and importance of Singapore theatre through marketing, communications, and relationship building. You will work closely with the Practice team to communicate our vision and achieve the company’s objectives.

Key Responsibilities     

  • As Practice’s in-house Graphic Designer (Visual Communications Executive), you will assist the Visual Communications Manager and work with cross-departments to develop designs and strategies to meet all creative needs of the company.

  • Be a creative mind behind Practice’s branding and its assets.

  • Conceptualise and execute the design of communication collaterals (e.g. show brochures, posters, banners, eDMs, programme booklets, front-of-house display etc.) while deepening your expertise and understanding in Singapore’s arts industry.

  • Manage multiple projects and each of their project strategy, timeline and budget to ensure design deliverables are met.

  • Liaise with external suppliers, such as printers, to ensure timely and quality delivery of materials.

Job Requirements

  • A university degree in graphic design/visual communications.

  • A candidate with at least 1 year’s working experience preferred; fresh grads are also welcomed.

  • Well-versed in Mac OS and devices with a strong proficiency in Adobe's Creative Cloud (Photoshop, Illustrator, In-design, Lightroom, Premiere Pro), Google Workspace and Microsoft (Word, PowerPoint).

  • Proven graphic design skills, backed by digital portfolio/personal website/blog/ Behance.

  • A candidate with photography, video editing, and illustration skills is a bonus.

  • Strong proficiency in English and Mandarin (Spoken and Written) to comprehend and design English and Chinese collaterals.

  • Good interpersonal skills and a team player.

  • Independent worker, with strong organisational skills and attention to detail.

  • Ability to work under pressure while managing multiple projects, timelines, deliverables and meeting deadlines.

  • Passion in the arts is an advantage.

If you are interested in this position, please email your CV to ttp@practice.org.sg.

postedCompany.description:
The Theatre Practice (Practice) is Singapore’s longest-standing professional bilingual theatre institution. Artist-driven and deeply progressive, Practice strives to be an arts space in Singapore that consciously nurtures and empowers people who care about humanity. Over the past 53 years, we have developed and presented works that aim to reflect the richness and complexities of Singapore’s diverse cultures.

Today, we carry on the work of our visionary co-founders and cultural medallion recipients, Kuo Pao Kun and Goh Lay Kuan. Under the helm of current Artistic Director Kuo Jian Hong, Practice continues to shape Singapore’s cultural landscape as the definitive voice in Singapore theatre. Jian Hong is deeply influenced by the importance of play in art-making and under her leadership, Practice has become the embodiment of the irrepressible spirit and fearless experimentation of play.

Practice is motivated by a desire to always respectfully entertain. We have collaborated with Singapore and international artists alike to present a critically-acclaimed repertoire of over 330 productions including musicals, classics, original and experimental works. We have toured extensively in the region. Many of our works have entered the canon of Southeast Asian literature and have been adapted and presented by theatre companies worldwide, presenting an authentically Singaporean voice on the world stage.

Since our inception, Practice has served as a cultural bridge between Asia and the world. Our current theatre festival – M1 Patch! A Theatre Festival of Artful Play – comes from a legacy of carefully curated festivals and seminars that gather practitioners of all disciplines and cultures for sharing and exchange, while providing a valuable platform for outstanding international works.

As pioneers in Arts Education in Singapore, Practice has been instrumental both in nurturing leaders in the Singapore arts scene, and inculcating a love for the arts in generations of Singapore youth.

Artistically diverse and constantly relevant, Practice will always engage with the ceaseless exploration of new frontiers.

The Theatre Practice is an Institution of Public Character, a recipient of the National Arts Council’s Major Grant 2017-2020, and a member of the Singapore Chinese Language Theatre Alliance.
postedCompany.name: THE THEATRE PRACTICE LTD.
